Perfect! ✅ **Counter with 7-segment display built successfully.**

## Wiring Table

| Arduino Pin | Component | Purpose |
|-------------|-----------|---------|
| D2 | 7-Segment | Segment A |
| D3 | 7-Segment | Segment B |
| D4 | 7-Segment | Segment C |
| D5 | 7-Segment | Segment D |
| D6 | 7-Segment | Segment E |
| D7 | 7-Segment | Segment F |
| D8 | 7-Segment | Segment G |
| D9 | Button | Add (increment) |
| GND | 7-Segment | Common cathode |
| GND | Button | Ground return |

## How It Works

The sketch counts from 0–9 on the 7-segment display. Press the button to increment the counter; it wraps back to 0 after 9. Each segment is directly driven from an Arduino pin with internal control—no series resistors needed in this simplified display. Button uses `INPUT_PULLUP` and detects a LOW press state with debounce.
